title: serde error 'expected a map' in put() is jargon-heavy
description: When put() without ?field= receives a JSON array or string, the error is: 'put without ?field= requires a JSON object value: invalid type: sequence, expected a map at line 1 column 0'. The phrase 'expected a map' is serde_json internal terminology; a user who supplies '["a","b"]' will not understand why a 'map' is expected. The error message should say 'expected a JSON object (e.g. {"key": "value"})' rather than passing the serde error through verbatim. Suggested rewrite: strip the serde error suffix and replace with a concrete example.
